transcript.text: Pretty good.
Can't complain.
As a heads up, Steve's not going to be joining today.
We do have a new meeting recorder in here.
this is like we just worked on getting CRM called ATEO, which records meetings.
So that's why there's currently two people in here and three recorders as well as a transcriber that you don't see.
I know.
It's so funny.
Like there are just so many tools out there.
And we similarly have been testing Fireflies versus Fathom.
So we've had meetings where there's like, you know, two people and six different recording tools.
The ones that you can actually see.
I have granola running in the background.
Well, welcome.
Welcome robots to the meeting.
Yeah.
assume you won't even be necessary.
It's just going to be, I'll just have my bot talk to you here about it.
Yeah, exactly.
You don't even need to come.
Hey, yeah.
Nice talking to you.
Appreciate you making the time.
Hope you're doing well.
Yeah, you too.
I'm going to share.
Our weekly agenda.
Keep me honest.
So I think in terms of content, actually, you know what?
I'm going to share something different.
I want to just touch base on the calibration articles and see, you know, how you're feeling, any high-level feedback.

I know Steve is taking a look at the LandGraph one, which I think will be really helpful from kind of a technical perspective to sort of know how we're doing there and see if we need updates kind of to our documentation or kind of any approach to building the pipeline, because I know that sometimes more technical content, we need to kind of add more stuff.
So, talk to Steve this morning.
He's reviewed it, and he's good with it.
He's quite happy with it, actually.
He seems that it's
Founded in a really good place.
So I think we're good to go with that.
I'm actually also quite happy with it.
I really like it.
I think that it's going to be a good thing for us to build off of going forward.
I do think, now that I see this one in front of you, in front of us again, that the changes that we made there might want us to make some changes over here.
Specifically, a lot of what we ended up changing was this distinction of build versus buy.
And is that the right distinction?
Or should we frame it as managing your own infrastructure versus sort of offloading it?
And the example that Steve uses a lot that I like is like, every business now still needs servers, but they offload it to AWS instead of managing their own servers.
Like, can we be that for, like, AI agents essentially?
We're the AWS.
But no one would say that I want to build versus buy servers.
Like, that's like a strange, like, business thing to make.
So I think like that build versus buy thing is sort of, I don't know, it's like business-y.
think, like, marketing and, like, content and, like, salesy folks like that distinction more.
And so it doesn't change much as far as the content, but I think the framing is important.

And so I kind of want to push us to take some of those learning from the last article that we just want some time kind of trying to perfect and get the supply to this article as well, if that's something that you're cool with.
Yeah, of course.
Yeah, I think we can definitely take a look at this one with that lens.
And then we are going to deliver five articles this week, too.
So I think with that, you'll also see sometimes I think some of those framing questions, it's easier to see across a larger content base.
So I think if you see anything reviewing this next round of five articles, too, we can kind of continue to calibrate on that as well.
But I think it's a good, yeah, it's a very good call out to think about, you know, how we're how we're framing these.
And I think that build versus by distinction is like a really good example.

And I like that the AWS example that you just shared, I feel like something like that, even using that as an example, I think could be really useful to put into the artifacts themselves some guidance around approach.
Yeah, yeah, no, for sure.
And I think we're just, because my last role was I was working in a company called DBT that we, part of what we did was we had like a SaaS version of a free open source product.
And I think that's where like build versus buy makes sense.
It's like when it's the exact same product that you can either rule the open source version for free, or you can pay us to manage it for you.
That's when it's hard to make sense.

But when it's like two different levels of abstraction and two different kinds of like outcomes, or a few different kinds of like approaches, I guess, then I think folks don't tend to use this language as much.
So yeah, I will the changes that we're making to these calibration articles, be reflected on those five that are coming this week?
Or is it kind of like coming?
Thank you.
No, they should be, I think, especially any of the feedback that you got us last week should be reflected.
I think I asked Rachel if she could deliver them on a rolling basis instead of kind of like as a chunk.

That way, I think if there were any big call outs from Steve or on this first article that she sent yesterday, we'd be able to kind of make those more incremental changes because the goal is that we don't get behind on updates so that then we're going back and like re-updating big batches of content that we can kind of calibrate as we go in a useful way.
So, yeah, I will let her know about specifically this calibration article that we should do one more update on that.
And let me also, I can just flag that the one she sent yesterday, if there's any feedback that's, I don't know if Steve left feedback in the Landgraf article itself.
No, he just reviewed it and did not leave it on Slack and he's happy with it.
Yeah, so then all of the...
All of the feedback that we captured last week should be reflected in this most recent batch of articles.
cool.
I know there were changes that I was making to it like as of yesterday or the day before.
So I guess kind of my request would be like I'm fine if there's fewer than five this week.
I just want to make sure that the calibration that we're doing is like well reflected and that's a higher priority for me right now.
And then hopefully next week we can get cranking.
Yeah, 100%.

So I'll touch base with Rachel, especially if you were leaving feedback yesterday to make sure that that would be captured in what she delivered yesterday and just let her know that we want to focus on getting those artifacts really tight.
And we're focusing on quality over quantity.
Well, in general, but especially this week.
Yeah, absolutely.
And then as well as if we could try to maybe do another crank at this first calibration article about document extraction to reflect detainers from the second one onto the first one.
Definitely.
And then the other thing I wanted, I think I dropped it in my update, but I might have gotten lost, is that we did work.
on adding an updated image step to the pipeline based on your new style.
So I've added those to both the calibration articles.

So if you want to take a look at those and just give us any feedback you have, I mean, it can be as high level as, you know, we like these ones, we don't like these ones, and, you know, I'm not sure why.
I mean, first, I think that these look really cool.
I don't know why there's like an X in the top right of every image.
You know, it's just because the, it's literally because the down, it wasn't working to download it from Atlas for some reason, so I just screenshotted them to get them okay, cool.
I thought it was funny.
yeah, ignore those.
Yeah.
Yeah, no, these look good.
I want to maybe take a minute to reflect on them in a meeting on them.
Yeah, 100%.
But no issues at the moment.
So right now they're in both of the calibration articles in the new tab, and I'll make sure they're in the new one as well.
So just throw in any feedback you have on those.
And would there be, like, are these...
Is there, like, sub-images as in, like, does it not be the header image of the article?
This would be, like, within the content itself.
Is that correct?
I mean, I think it's a little bit up to you how you want to apply this new style to your blog post.
Because I know on your, I can't remember, I know you've been using them on the sub-stack right now.
So I think that connection, I know you've been doing the header images yourselves.
So, you know, our plan right now is to, our pipeline produces a standard of, like, five per, five images per post.
So we can, like, use some of those in line if you want to, I wasn't sure how you want to approach, like, header image versus inline images in your blog.
The way I think about it is, like, the header images, like, obviously it's a must, we need to have one for sure.
And then I would love to have inline images at some defined frequency.
That we discussed, this is like either once every 500 words or 200 or something like that.
I don't want us to like overdo the inline images where it like kind of interferes with the content.
But I do want to make sure that, yeah, we are breaking up the text a little bit.
kind of the main goal.
And I think that clarifying which one is meant to be which, which one is the header image versus which one is inline content will kind of help us make sure if they're good enough.
Because ideally, the one that's the header image is like not just reflecting the contents of one paragraph or it's like actually a broader like kind of theme.
So I do think that it's helpful to separate whatever that one is out from the rest of them.
Yeah.
So these ones are pulling from the overall are like this pipeline is using kind of the overall article topic versus kind of specific sections.
So I would say we would think about these probably as the hero image, the header image.
you much.
Thank Thank you, much.

But I guess my only question there would be if you want like a different style or like a more simplified image for the header image or if you kind of like this more watercolor editorial style for the header image as well.
I think the same kind of style would work.
I'm curious, like I before we like actually double down on this exact style, I know this is the style that we've done in our sub stack.
I would love to just like try a few different styles and make sure we feel like this is the right one that we want to go down the road with.

Like I don't want us to feel like just because this is where we landed on sub stack as we were trying to move super fast like last month is what we should commit to for the next year for like our business.
So is it like possible that we pause and try a couple of different things here?
So when I brought this up, I was like, this would be great if we did it.
And I hope I didn't.
I think I may have like made it sound like it's like.
Exactly what we want to do.
To me, this is like a benchmark of like hand-drawn, like look and feel.
It doesn't feel super AI sloppy.
That is still like scalable.
But if there's other examples of that kind of style, then I want to at least like explore them before we commit.
Yeah, for sure.
I mean, I think the reality is we can test anything.
Like if you have other examples of things you like, I'm happy to share them with our designer.
And get her to put together some examples.
I would say based on like what we're working on across clients and what I see out there, I do feel like this kind of hand-drawn approach feels different than what's out there.
I feel like I can think of a ton of examples of clients who are like using stock imagery in various ways or using kind of more kind of like computer generated, but very like tech sort of image.

I think there are some more that look like a little bit more like vectors and sort of probably halfway between that, but I wouldn't say this like hand-drawn style is something that feels like something I've seen across the other accounts we're working on, but I'm happy to pull together just some comps of like some of the other things we're working on.
I think any kind of comp would be helpful to make me and the team feel like we explored our options and we like, we didn't just like happen to choose the one that we were already using.
But yeah, if I do trust what you're saying around, it's likely this is maybe where we land ultimately.
Yeah, for sure.
And I'll also, I'll look and see a million accounts I'm not working on too, like everything that was changing, right?
So I'll see if I can source some examples because I would be curious to see if anyone else is working on something kind of like this because for me it feels unique.
And different from the other accounts I'm working on and how they're treating images.
So, yeah, I'm happy to pull together some examples so just so can get a sense of what else is out there.
Cool.
Sounds good.
And, again, if nothing else works out, I would feel pretty comfortable moving forward with this.
Yeah.
And honestly, and if you just see stuff out there from other companies, too, like, pull it as examples.
Like, if you just see stuff that you like.
Because we like inspiration.
All right.
Okay.
So I think we're good there.
I think we're working on getting the Logic workflow and the auto-publishing steps in.
Again, that won't affect anything you see.
That's just kind of streamlining from our end.
And I also have, I haven't forgotten about updating the existing articles.
I feel like focusing on getting us back on a regular publishing schedule at five a week has felt like the priority here.
And then I think we can go back and look at those other ones, especially once we feel like we've really calibrated and nailed the artifacts and the way we're talking about Logic.
So, oops, I meant to take that out.
know we touched base on that last week.
So I will say I think I was going to just send like a reporting overview.
I know we've sort of fallen off on reporting as we're doing this work, but I feel like it's useful to see kind of what's going on with traffic over the last couple months anyway.
And then the only other thing for planning I wanted to talk about is that I know kind of AEO is a big focus for you, and we've sort of been using Scrunch to track that.
But GrowthX has been kind of in the background working on our own AI visibility tracking platform to monitor how AI engines are kind of citing and mentioning brands.
And we've, ours is a little different.
There's a few things that are different about it.
But I think the biggest difference is that we're really focusing on B2B buying categories.
And so the prompts we're using, the way we're tracking categories is like much more focused on the buyer's journey there.
So it's been informing strategy in the background.
But over the next couple of weeks, we're going to roll it out so that clients have access in it.
So you'd be able to take a look and be able to track, to be able to update prompts that are being tracked for your brand and just get more visibility.
So my hope is that next week, I'll be able, or next time we meet, I'll be able to demo that for you.
And then we'll kind of be rolling it out to give you direct access to that.
So that's the exciting news.
That's awesome.
Yeah, I'm stoked.
I'm looking forward to seeing that.
Thank you so much.
Yeah, and one of the, I think my favorite features is that things that like Scrunch doesn't have is like a historical possibility.
So when you start tracking like categories and questions.

get a couple of months of data versus kind of like starting to track it at that moment, which I feel like makes such a big difference for moments like this where you decide to pivot and then you're not just kind of like starting totally from scratch.
yeah, no, that's dope.
That's awesome.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So more to come on that, but just wanted to give you a peek at that since I know it's something that you all are particularly interested in.
Yes, we for sure appreciate that.
Thank you.
I do have a couple more things on my end that maybe I should have added to the agenda.
No, yeah, lay them on me.
Cool.
Two things.
One of them, it was actually regarding the Lang Graph Calibration article.
I guess the question I had was like, is that too long?
Like, should I be thinking about like length at all?
Are y'all thinking about it?
Like, I know that just give you that like longer is like beneficial when it comes to like SEO and stuff.
Yeah.
But the sense I got after reading it again this morning was that we probably could cut like the last or some portion of the last half and not have it lose too much value.
So I was just curious, is that a thing that y'all are assessing for?
Yeah, I think it's definitely something we're looking at.

I think this is like pushing length, but I think that I don't, sorry, I mean, worry is extreme, but I think when things get over 3,000 words, I think the biggest thing I look at is when things get long is more looking at the content itself.
Like, is there a lot of filler in there?
Is doing a good job answering the question and fulfilling search intent?
Because I think that really it all comes down to quality.
So it would be much more to me a signal that like, is the quality still there if we have these really long articles?
I do think that with everybody starting to add FAQs, like content is just getting longer.
But we have clients, like I have one client who's regularly pushing articles that like publishing that are like almost 4,000 words and we haven't seen
I it affect, like, ranking or traffic at all.
So I would say something to keep an eye on, but I'm not, I wouldn't say I'm concerned as long as the quality bar remains.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I I guess when the content is no longer exclusively meant for, like, human consumption, then the structure of it, I guess, changes a little bit.
And the thing I wanted make sure that we're not doing was, like, as that is a calibration article, using that as, like, the benchmark for, like, length of an article.
And I'm making sure I'm having all of our future ones be 3,000 plus words long as well, just because that one was.
Yeah, no, definitely not.
We wouldn't be using that as a, like, a necessary, like, guide.
You know, I think a lot of these two are based on what the SERP looks like, too.
So, like, some types of articles tend to be longer than others just because that's also the type of content that's ranking there.
So, I think, like, our benchmark should be quality and also whether or not the topic itself.
Requires that kind of length, but yeah, we wouldn't be, that's not like our new guide of, you know, 3,000 words per article or anything.
Just making sure.
Okay, cool.

The other thing was we did like this huge like annual, like quarterly product planning thing this last week in which we sort of like laid out a vision for how we want our product to expand over the next quarter.

And like super like TLDR version of it is we want to be like a full featured agent builder that reaches out into other tools, academic actions across your docs and your CRM and, you know, can take in more different kinds of document files, can handle more types of media and, you know, just like it does all the things that you want an agent builder to do essentially.
And one of the thoughts that I had there was, as we add out new capabilities in the product, that probably should change the keywords that we're targeting on SEO.
For instance, we, as of December now do voice processing as well.
We don't have any customers really using it in production yet.
But we could theoretically handle phone calls, files and transcribe them and answer questions about them, things of that nature.
And just as we include document processing in our search terms, we should do that ideally for voice processing as well.
And as we add more features in the future, keep adding those to our roadmap as well.
So no super strong action required right now.
I wanted to kind of raise for you that over the next three months, we're going to be launching new stuff relatively fast.
And each time we do that, I'd like to just brief you all and see how that's going to impact our kind of keyword or search term target list as well.
Yeah, 100%.
I mean, I think that's exactly, we should be shifting our strategy.
And sometimes that might be as simple as doing a new round of keyword research.
And sometimes it may change it more, but we should be updating our strategy regularly to kind of match new product features on any new focus that you have.
Do you have documentation?
documentation from that that we could use to kind of
Take a look at our current strategy and keywords, because I think whatever you have now, we can plan based on that and then kind of plan to update it regularly.
We need to make sure that we're good to share that with you all.
Otherwise, I can just share the whole strategy document.
I don't think there's anything.
Obviously, is like a sort of look before, like top secret, I guess.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Confidential is the right word, but I don't think it should be something that is not okay for you all to see.
So I'll just make sure that it's cool, then I'll share it with you all soon.
Yeah, because if that's great, then I think what we should do is just plan to – because, I mean, as we develop this strategy, we were sort of doing it as you were making the changes.
So I think it would be good to look at what we have so far and just do a check to make sure that, yeah, it captures everything that's in there and see where we need to expand or change direction.
Sweet.
Yeah, those were the two things on my end.
Awesome.
So, yeah, if you want to share whenever you're ready, you can share that.
And if
If we need to have sort of a follow-up conversation, we can do that at our next meeting as well, if we have any questions on that.
Otherwise, we'll plan to just take that and kind of take a look at the strategy, and if we have recommendations for any shifts or expanded keyword research, we can do that as well.
Cool.
Sounds good.
Thank you so much.
All right.
Awesome.
Thanks so much.
Have a good day.
See you then.
Take care.
All right.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content calibration, product roadmap, and AEO tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=195.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Framing:** The "build vs. buy" framing will be replaced with "managing your own infrastructure vs. offloading it" (e.g., Logic as the "AWS for AI agents") to better resonate with a technical audience.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
  - [**Quality Over Quantity:** This week's content output will be reduced from 5 articles to prioritize quality and fully integrate calibration feedback, ensuring new articles reflect the updated framing.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=490.0)
  - [**Image Style Exploration:** The current "hand-drawn" image style is a benchmark, not a final decision. GrowthX will provide comps of alternative styles to ensure the team explores all options before committing.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
  - [**AEO Tracking Upgrade:** GrowthX is launching a new AEO tracking platform with historical data and B2B-focused prompts, addressing a key limitation of the current tool, Scrunch.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=990.0)

## Topics

### Content Calibration & Framing

  - [**"Build vs. Buy" Framing:**](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
      - [The current framing is too "business-y" and less effective for a technical audience.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
      - [**New Framing:** "Managing your own infrastructure vs. offloading it."](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This aligns with how technical users think about services like AWS, where the decision is about operational management, not building from scratch.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=267.0)
  - [**Implementation:**](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=409.0)
      - [The new framing will be applied to the first calibration article (document extraction) and all subsequent content.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=409.0)
      - [GrowthX will ensure this feedback is integrated into the 5 articles planned for this week by communicating directly with the writer, Rachel.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=490.0)

### Image Style & Strategy

  - [**Current Style:** The "hand-drawn" style is a good benchmark for a scalable, non-"AI sloppy" look.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=771.0)
  - [**Decision:** The team will explore other styles before committing to this one long-term.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The current style was adopted quickly for Substack and may not be the optimal choice for the business's long-term brand.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
  - [**Action:** GrowthX will provide comps of alternative styles for review.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Usage:**](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=597.0)
      - [**Header Images:** Must reflect the article's broad theme.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=680.0)
      - [**Inline Images:** Used to break up text at a defined frequency (e.g., every 200-500 words).](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=647.0)

### Product Roadmap & SEO

  - [**Product Vision:** Logic is expanding into a full-featured agent builder with integrations (docs, CRM), more file types, and voice processing.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1267.0)
  - [**SEO Impact:** New features require updating the SEO strategy to target relevant keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1303.0)
      - [**Example:** Voice processing, launched in December, needs to be added to the keyword list.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1303.0)
  - [**Action:** Azzam will share the product roadmap document with GrowthX to inform a strategy review and keyword research update.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1375.0)

### AEO Tracking Platform

  - [**Problem:** The current tool, Scrunch, lacks historical data, which makes tracking performance changes after a strategic pivot difficult.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1068.0)
  - [**Solution:** GrowthX is launching a new, proprietary AEO tracking platform.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=990.0)
      - [**Key Features:**](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1068.0)
          - [Historical data tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1068.0)
          - [B2B-focused prompts tailored to the buyer's journey.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=990.0)
          - [Client access to update tracked prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)
  - [**Timeline:** GrowthX will demo the platform next week and roll out client access soon after.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
      - [Update the document extraction calibration article with the new framing.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [Communicate the "quality over quantity" directive to Rachel for this week's articles.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=490.0)
      - [Provide comps of alternative image styles for review.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
      - [Demo the new AEO tracking platform next week.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1053.0)
  - [**Azzam:**](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1375.0)
      - [Share the product roadmap document with GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/KyRCzyGridC5ditnp5ys7nAykP4m8zyL?tab=summary&timestamp=1375.0)

action_items.description: Email Rachel re: apply build-vs-buy framing + AWS example to calibration articles
Email Rachel re: capture Azzam's yesterday's feedback in today's deliveries
Email Rachel re: update Document Extraction calibration article using learnings from LangGraph
Add updated image step to new calibration article
Email Azzam image-style comps + external examples; request Logic examples
Email Azzam reporting overview (last few months)
Demo AI visibility tracking platform to Azzam next meeting
Confirm w/ internal stakeholders; email Bailey product strategy doc
